channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 Grants Offer Up to €1 Million for NGOs
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 call for proposals is now open, offering grants of up to €1 million for eligible non-profit organisations. This major European Union funding opportunity is designed for civil society organisations, NGOs, and development actors working to strengthen democratic participation, human rights, media freedom, and […]

The post Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 Grants Offer Up to €1 Million for NGOs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 call for proposals is now open, offering grants of up to €1 million for eligible non-profit organisations. This major European Union funding opportunity is designed for civil society organisations, NGOs, and development actors working to strengthen democratic participation, human rights, media freedom, and local development in Jordan.

With a total budget of €3 million, the programme provides significant support for organisations ready to scale their impact, build stronger partnerships, and contribute to inclusive social progress. For organisations seeking substantial funding to deliver long-term initiatives in Jordan, this is an opportunity worth serious attention.

About the Program

The call is managed by the European Commission and aims to support an independent, participatory, and empowered civil society in Jordan. It is divided into two funding lots, each targeting an important area of development.

Lot 1: Support to Media and Freedom of Expression has an indicative allocation of €2 million. This lot focuses on strengthening independent media, protecting freedom of expression, and supporting open civic space.

Lot 2: Civil Society Support to Local Development has an indicative allocation of €1 million. It is intended to strengthen civil society organisations as contributors to governance, development planning, and community-level progress.

Successful proposals must take place in Jordan and run for 24 to 36 months.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Civil society organisations often face funding constraints when trying to deliver sustainable, large-scale impact. This programme offers not only financial support but also strategic backing from the European Union for organisations working in areas essential to democratic development.

For Jordan-based organisations, this can help expand services, strengthen advocacy, improve community engagement, and create long-term institutional capacity. For international organisations partnering with local actors, the programme offers a valuable route to collaborative and locally grounded development work.

At a time when civic participation, accountable governance, and inclusive development remain global priorities, this funding can help organisations turn ideas into measurable action.

What Participants Will Gain

Successful applicants can gain far more than grant funding through this programme.

First, organisations can access substantial financial support, with grants between €900,000 and €1,000,000. This level of funding enables organisations to implement ambitious multi-year projects with real scale.

Second, applicants can strengthen their institutional credibility by partnering on an EU-funded initiative. This can improve future fundraising prospects and increase visibility among donors and stakeholders.

Third, the programme encourages partnership-building between local and international organisations, creating opportunities for knowledge exchange and long-term collaboration.

Finally, funded organisations can generate lasting social impact by supporting rights-based initiatives, stronger communities, and more inclusive governance systems.

Who Can Apply

Eligible lead applicants must be:

  • Legal persons
  • Non-profit organisations
  • Non-governmental organisations or similar civil society entities
  • Effectively established in Jordan or an EU Member State

Applicants may apply individually or together with co-applicants and affiliated entities, provided they meet the programme requirements.

Organisations should also demonstrate the ability to directly manage projects and deliver results.

Eligible Areas

Projects funded under this programme must take place in Jordan.

The initiative is especially relevant for organisations working in:

  • Human rights
  • Media development
  • Freedom of expression
  • Civic participation
  • Governance reform
  • Community development
  • Youth engagement
  • Capacity building for local organisations

Application Process and Deadline

This is a restricted call for proposals. In the first stage, applicants must submit a concept note through the European Commission’s PROSPECT online portal. Shortlisted organisations will then be invited to submit a full application.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early, review eligibility criteria carefully, and ensure organisational registrations are completed in advance.

Application Deadline: 14 June 2026 (12:00 Brussels time)

Learn more and apply on the official European Commission website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 Grants Offer Up to €1 Million for NGOs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027: Paid $15,000 Internship for Graduate Students in Research and Evaluation
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027 is a paid professional development opportunity for graduate students in the United States who want to build careers in research, evaluation, and culturally responsive practice. Offered by the American Evaluation Association, the program provides a $15,000 stipend, hands-on internship experience, mentorship, training seminars, and access to […]

The post AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027: Paid $15,000 Internship for Graduate Students in Research and Evaluation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027 is a paid professional development opportunity for graduate students in the United States who want to build careers in research, evaluation, and culturally responsive practice. Offered by the American Evaluation Association, the program provides a $15,000 stipend, hands-on internship experience, mentorship, training seminars, and access to a national network of evaluation professionals.

Designed to support students from historically underrepresented backgrounds, GEDI helps emerging scholars strengthen their research skills while gaining practical experience in the growing field of evaluation.

About the Program

The GEDI Program runs from September 2026 through July 2027 and combines academic learning with applied professional experience. Scholars complete the equivalent of approximately two days per week at an internship placement, usually located near their graduate institution.

Interns work with agencies and organizations on real evaluation projects that may involve assessing programs, analyzing outcomes, measuring community impact, or supporting evidence-based decision-making. These placements are structured to help participants apply their graduate training to real-world challenges.

In addition to the internship placement, scholars participate in seminars, webinars, networking events, and national conference activities throughout the academic year.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many graduate students build strong research skills but have limited exposure to evaluation as a career path. The GEDI Program bridges that gap by helping students transfer academic research knowledge into professional evaluation practice.

Evaluation professionals play a critical role across sectors including public health, education, government, nonprofits, philanthropy, and social impact organizations. They help institutions understand what works, what needs improvement, and how programs can better serve communities.

GEDI also places a strong emphasis on culturally responsive evaluation, helping scholars think critically about race, culture, equity, language, and inclusion in research design and practice. This makes the program especially valuable for students interested in ethical and community-centered careers.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected scholars receive a $15,000 stipend in recognition of their internship work and successful completion of program requirements. Major travel costs for required in-person components, including airfare and shared hotel accommodations, are also reimbursed.

Participants gain direct evaluation experience through a supervised internship placement connected to their field of interest. They also receive mentorship from experienced professionals, guidance from program leadership, and support from an academic advisor.

The program includes training seminars in Washington, D.C., attendance at the Eval2026 Conference in Charlotte, North Carolina, monthly webinars, and networking opportunities with leaders in evaluation.

By the end of the program, scholars will have practical work experience, professional connections, stronger applied research skills, and a clearer pathway into careers in evaluation and impact measurement.

Who Can Apply

The GEDI Program is open to students who are currently enrolled in a master’s or doctoral program in the United States and have completed at least one full year of graduate-level coursework.

Applicants should have prior exposure to research methods and demonstrate a strong interest in applying research skills to evaluation. Candidates must also show commitment to culturally responsive practice and be eligible to work for pay in the United States.

The program is particularly focused on supporting graduate students of color and individuals from historically underrepresented backgrounds.

Eligible Areas

Applicants must be residing in the United States during the program period and enrolled in a U.S.-based graduate institution.

Students from a wide range of disciplines are encouraged to apply, including public health, education, sociology, psychology, anthropology, political science, social work, and related fields.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants should prepare essays, a professional reference letter, academic advisor support, and a CV as part of the application process. Finalists may be invited for interviews.

The deadline to apply for the GEDI Program 2026–2027 is 15 May 2026.

Graduate students interested in paid professional experience, mentorship, and a pathway into evaluation careers should strongly consider applying.

Learn more and apply on the official AEA website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027: Paid $15,000 Internship for Graduate Students in Research and Evaluation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Conservation Futures Intern in South Africa 2026 (Paid Internship with Arizona State University & Conservation International)
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
If you are passionate about environmental sustainability, wildlife conservation, and community development, the Conservation Futures Internship 2026 offers a rare and transformative opportunity to gain hands-on experience in one of Africa’s most important ecological landscapes. Organized by Conservation International in partnership with Arizona State University, this immersive internship combines fieldwork, academic learning, and professional development […]

The post Conservation Futures Intern in South Africa 2026 (Paid Internship with Arizona State University & Conservation International) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about environmental sustainability, wildlife conservation, and community development, the Conservation Futures Internship 2026 offers a rare and transformative opportunity to gain hands-on experience in one of Africa’s most important ecological landscapes. Organized by Conservation International in partnership with Arizona State University, this immersive internship combines fieldwork, academic learning, and professional development in South Africa.

Set within the breathtaking Kruger to Canyons Landscape, this program places interns at the heart of real-world conservation challenges and solutions—making it an ideal stepping stone for future conservation leaders.

About the Conservation Futures Internship

The Conservation Futures Internship is a three-month, paid, full-time field-based program designed to equip students with practical experience in conservation and community engagement.

 Location

  • Acornhoek, Limpopo Province, South Africa
  • Near the iconic Kruger National Park

Internship Duration

  • Start Date: June 1, 2026
  • End Date: August 31, 2026

Application Deadline

  • May 15, 2026 (11:49 PM EST)

What Makes This Internship Unique?

This is not a typical internship—it is a deeply immersive, field-driven experience that blends science, culture, and community.

Key Highlights

  • Paid Internship: Financial support provided
  • Travel Support: In-country travel expenses covered
  • Free Accommodation: Housing near the work site
  • Transport Provided: Daily commute arranged
  • Global Certification: Certificate from Arizona State University upon completion
  • Online Courses: Free academic training alongside fieldwork
  • Mentorship: Guidance from experienced conservation professionals

Work You Will Be Doing

Interns will actively contribute to conservation and community-based initiatives. Responsibilities may vary depending on the project site but typically include:

1. Conservation Field Support

  • Assist in wildlife monitoring and habitat restoration
  • Participate in planting and ecological restoration activities
  • Conduct field assessments and biodiversity tracking
  • Support conservation agreements with local stakeholders

2. Community Engagement

  • Work closely with local and Indigenous communities
  • Support workshops, meetings, and outreach programs
  • Document how conservation aligns with community livelihoods

3. Research & Documentation

  • Collect and organize field data
  • Capture real-life conservation stories through writing and visuals
  • Contribute to reports, presentations, and learning materials

4. Innovation & Communication

  • Help develop creative solutions for conservation challenges
  • Collaborate with teams to improve outcomes for both people and nature

5. Professional Development

  • Participate in mentorship sessions and cohort learning
  • Reflect on personal and professional growth
  • Build cross-cultural and fieldwork skills

Working Conditions

This internship is designed for individuals ready for a real-world experience:

  • Significant time spent outdoors in rural and remote environments
  • Exposure to basic living conditions
  • Frequent interaction with local communities
  • Local travel within the project area (transport provided)

Learning Experience with Arizona State University

A standout feature of this program is the integration of academic learning:

  • Interns will complete online courses through Arizona State University’s School of Conservation Futures
  • Courses are fully funded (free of charge)
  • Upon successful completion, participants receive a professional certificate

This dual approach ensures that interns gain both practical skills and theoretical knowledge.

Eligibility Criteria

Required Qualifications

  • Enrollment at Arizona State University
  • Academic background in:
    • Conservation
    • Environmental Studies
    • Community Development
    • Or related fields
  • Strong interest in real-world conservation
  • Willingness to live and work in remote areas
  • Cultural sensitivity and teamwork skills
  • Adaptability and a proactive mindset

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in:
    • Fieldwork
    • Community engagement
    • Volunteer programs
  • Skills in:
    • Ecology
    • Natural resource management
    • Communications or storytelling
  • Interest in:
    • Wildlife conservation
    • Habitat restoration
    • Human-wildlife relationships
  • Familiarity with Southern African contexts (an advantage)

How To Apply.

Click Here for more and to apply

Applications are reviewed on merit, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Why You Should Apply

This internship is ideal for students who want to:

  • Gain hands-on conservation experience
  • Work in one of Africa’s most biodiverse regions
  • Learn directly from experts and local communities
  • Build a global professional network
  • Develop skills that are highly valued in sustainability careers

Beyond the professional benefits, this experience offers a chance to make a tangible impact on both environmental conservation and community well-being.

Equal Opportunity Commitment

Conservation International is committed to fair and inclusive hiring practices, ensuring equal opportunity for all applicants regardless of background, in alignment with South African employment equity laws.

For more global internship opportunities Click here.

The post Conservation Futures Intern in South Africa 2026 (Paid Internship with Arizona State University & Conservation International) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Royal Academy of Engineering Policy Fellowships 2026
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Royal Academy of Engineering (RAEng) Policy Fellowships Programme is one of the most prestigious professional development opportunities designed to bridge the gap between engineering expertise and public policymaking. This fellowship equips policymakers with advanced technical insights, systems-thinking skills, and access to a powerful interdisciplinary network of engineers, researchers, and industry experts. The programme is […]

The post Royal Academy of Engineering Policy Fellowships 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Royal Academy of Engineering (RAEng) Policy Fellowships Programme is one of the most prestigious professional development opportunities designed to bridge the gap between engineering expertise and public policymaking. This fellowship equips policymakers with advanced technical insights, systems-thinking skills, and access to a powerful interdisciplinary network of engineers, researchers, and industry experts.

The programme is structured to help policymakers tackle complex national and global challenges by integrating engineering solutions into policy frameworks. It is especially valuable in today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, where policymaking increasingly requires technical literacy and innovation-driven approaches.

According to official information, the programme fosters a “unique network of policymakers, engineers and other experts” working collaboratively to develop solutions to economic, social, and technical challenges.

Fellowship Overview and Structure

The RAEng Policy Fellowship runs over a four-month intensive programme, combining practical learning, expert engagement, and policy-focused research.

Core Programme Components

Participants benefit from:

  • One-on-one meetings with leading engineers and subject-matter experts
  • Systems-thinking coaching sessions
  • Workshops (both in-person and virtual)
  • Peer-to-peer collaboration sessions
  • Development of a personal policy-focused report
  • Presentation of findings to a cohort of fellows

Additionally, fellows join a long-term alumni network, gaining continued access to professional development opportunities, events, and collaborations.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants)

Target Audience

This fellowship is specifically designed for:

  • Policymakers and public servants
  • Professionals involved in public policy design or service delivery
  • Individuals working in organizations with a public service mission

Geographic Eligibility

  • Primarily UK-based applicants
  • Applicants must be working within the UK policy ecosystem or public service sector

This means that applicants from Africa, Asia, Europe, or other regions are not directly eligible unless they are currently based in the UK and working in a relevant policy role.

Qualifications and Requirements

To be considered competitive, applicants must meet the following criteria:

1. Professional Role and Impact

  • Must be actively involved in policymaking or public service
  • Should have the ability to influence policy decisions or implementation

2. Defined Policy Challenge

  • Applicants must present a clear and well-defined policy problem or technical challenge that can benefit from engineering insights

3. Application Vision

  • Must demonstrate how the fellowship will be applied to real-world policy impact
  • Should show clear expectations and learning outcomes

4. Employer Support (Mandatory)

  • Applicants must have:
    • A sponsoring manager
    • Employer commitment to fund participation
  • Self-funding is not allowed

5. Commitment to Programme

  • Must actively participate throughout the fellowship
  • Expected to contribute as a long-term alumni member

Key Benefits of the Fellowship

1. High-Level Networking

Participants connect with:

  • Leading engineers
  • Academic experts
  • Industry professionals

2. Policy Innovation Skills

The programme enhances:

  • Systems thinking
  • Problem-solving using engineering frameworks
  • Evidence-based policymaking

3. Career Advancement

Fellows gain:

  • Recognition in policy and engineering circles
  • Access to exclusive alumni opportunities
  • Invitations to high-level events and collaborations

4. Real-World Impact

Participants develop actionable solutions to real policy challenges, often influencing national or institutional decision-making.

Application Process (Step-by-Step)

Step 1: Register Online

Applicants must create a profile via the RAEng grant management system.

Step 2: Review Guidance

Carefully read the FAQ and application guidance materials before submission.

Step 3: Prepare Application

Include:

  • Defined policy challenge
  • Learning objectives
  • Employer endorsement

Step 4: Submit Application

Applications are submitted through the official online system.

Step 5: Optional Support Sessions

Applicants can attend weekly drop-in sessions for:

  • Application guidance
  • Feedback on policy challenges

Apply here

Important Notes for Applicants

  • This is a highly competitive fellowship with strict selection criteria
  • Employer sponsorship is non-negotiable
  • The programme is not open for independent or self-funded applicants
  • Strong applications clearly link engineering expertise to policy impact

Why This Fellowship Matters

The RAEng Policy Fellowships Programme is not just another professional course—it is a transformative opportunity for policymakers to rethink how policies are designed and implemented in a technology-driven world.

By integrating engineering principles into governance, fellows gain the tools needed to address:

  • Climate challenges
  • Infrastructure development
  • Digital transformation
  • Public health systems
  • Economic resilience

Deadline

20th May 2026

If you are a policymaker working in the UK and seeking to elevate your impact through technical expertise and interdisciplinary collaboration, the Royal Academy of Engineering Policy Fellowship is a powerful opportunity. However, due to its geographic restriction, international applicants (including those from Africa) must first secure a UK-based policy role to become eligible.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Royal Academy of Engineering Policy Fellowships 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded 2026–2027 Scholars at Risk (SAR) Academic Freedom Fellowship (Mellon-Supported)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The 2026–2027 Scholars at Risk (SAR) Academic Freedom Fellowship, supported by the Mellon Foundation, stands as one of the most impactful global research opportunities for scholars committed to defending intellectual independence and academic freedom. Hosted by Scholars at Risk, this fellowship brings together a diverse, international cohort of researchers to explore pressing issues affecting higher […]

The post Fully Funded 2026–2027 Scholars at Risk (SAR) Academic Freedom Fellowship (Mellon-Supported) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026–2027 Scholars at Risk (SAR) Academic Freedom Fellowship, supported by the Mellon Foundation, stands as one of the most impactful global research opportunities for scholars committed to defending intellectual independence and academic freedom. Hosted by Scholars at Risk, this fellowship brings together a diverse, international cohort of researchers to explore pressing issues affecting higher education systems worldwide.

About the Scholars at Risk (SAR) Network

Scholars at Risk is a global network of over 650 higher education institutions across more than 40 countries. Its mission is to protect scholars, promote academic freedom, and defend the right to think, question, and share ideas without fear .

Through fellowships like this Mellon-supported program, SAR actively contributes to:

  • Safeguarding scholars at risk
  • Advancing research on academic freedom
  • Building global academic solidarity

Fellowship Tracks

Applicants must select one of two specialized research tracks:

1. Global Mellon Academic Freedom Fellowship

  • Focus: Academic freedom issues outside the United States
  • Encourages comparative, regional, or country-specific research

2. United States Academic Freedom Fellowship

  • Focus: Academic freedom within the U.S.
  • Priority themes include:
    • University autonomy
    • Institutional neutrality
    • Academic freedom in science and student contexts

What the Fellowship Offers

This is not just a funding opportunity—it is a comprehensive academic development experience.

Key Benefits

  • Financial Support: ~$8,000 stipend for research and academic outputs
  • Remote Flexibility: Fully online participation from anywhere in the world
  • Global Networking: Engage with leading scholars and experts
  • Professional Development: Structured workshops and mentorship

Program Activities

  • Monthly online workshops and peer sessions
  • Research development support
  • Collaborative academic engagement
  • Participation in SAR’s global academic freedom community

Expected Outputs

Fellows are required to produce a significant academic or public-facing output, such as:

  • Peer-reviewed journal articles
  • Policy papers or research reports
  • University course designs
  • Workshops, webinars, or training programs

Additionally, fellows may contribute to collaborative outputs like:

  • Edited academic volumes
  • Special journal issues
  • SAR-curated research publications

Eligibility Criteria

Who Can Apply?

This opportunity is tailored for:

  • Early-career researchers worldwide
  • Scholars passionate about academic freedom and higher education policy

Academic Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD obtained within the last 8 years, OR
  • Be a doctoral candidate in the final stages of completion

Professional Requirements

  • Must have an active affiliation with a university, research institution, or academic organization
  • Demonstrate strong interest or experience in academic freedom or related fields

Targeted Applicants and Inclusion

The fellowship strongly encourages applications from:

  • Researchers from the Global South
  • Scholars from marginalized or underrepresented communities
  • Academics who are currently or previously at risk due to their work or identity

Importantly, personal experience of risk is not mandatory, but it is valued.

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • Open to applicants worldwide
  • No country restrictions
  • Strong encouragement for candidates from:
    • Africa
    • Asia
    • Latin America
    • Middle East
    • Other underrepresented regions

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a complete application package including:

  • Research abstract (250–500 words)
  • Detailed research plan and timeline (max 2 pages)
  • Updated CV (max 2 pages with references)

Applications must be compiled into a single PDF file for submission.

Apply here

Why This Fellowship Matters

The SAR Academic Freedom Fellowship goes beyond traditional academic funding. It represents a global movement to protect intellectual independence, especially in regions where academic expression is under threat.

Participants gain:

  • Visibility in international academic circles
  • Opportunities to influence policy and discourse
  • A platform to contribute to global academic freedom advocacy

Deadline

29th May 2026

The Mellon-supported Scholars at Risk Fellowship 2026–2027 is a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity for researchers who want to make a meaningful impact in higher education and human rights. With generous funding, global exposure, and a strong intellectual community, this fellowship is ideal for scholars ready to advance both their research careers and the global defense of academic freedom.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded 2026–2027 Scholars at Risk (SAR) Academic Freedom Fellowship (Mellon-Supported) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Studentships at UWE Bristol 2026
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
8If you are searching for a fully funded PhD scholarship in the United Kingdom, the studentship opportunities offered by University of the West of England (UWE Bristol) represent one of the most competitive and rewarding postgraduate research pathways available to both domestic and international candidates. Overview of UWE Bristol PhD Studentships The UWE Bristol Doctoral […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Studentships at UWE Bristol 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
8If you are searching for a fully funded PhD scholarship in the United Kingdom, the studentship opportunities offered by University of the West of England (UWE Bristol) represent one of the most competitive and rewarding postgraduate research pathways available to both domestic and international candidates.

Overview of UWE Bristol PhD Studentships

The UWE Bristol Doctoral Academy offers a wide range of PhD studentships across multiple disciplines, designed to support high-achieving graduates in conducting cutting-edge research.

A studentship is a funded doctoral position where the university provides financial support while the student undertakes a specific research project. These opportunities are highly structured, meaning applicants apply for predefined research topics rather than proposing entirely independent research (in most cases).

What Does the Studentship Cover?

One of the strongest advantages of UWE Bristol studentships is the comprehensive financial package, which typically includes:

  • Full tuition fee coverage for up to three years
  • A tax-free monthly stipend for living expenses
  • Access to research facilities and academic supervision
  • Potential additional funding depending on the project

These benefits make the programme particularly attractive for international students and those without independent funding.

Available Fields and Research Areas

UWE Bristol offers studentships across its three major academic colleges:

1. College of Arts, Technology and Environment

Research areas include:

  • Cyber Security
  • Wearable Robotics
  • Sustainable Architecture and Energy Systems

2. College of Business and Law

Research topics may include:

  • Digital marketing and consumer behavior
  • International law and misinformation
  • Workplace inclusion and HR innovation

3. College of Health, Science and Society

Research areas include:

  • Healthcare workforce development
  • Environmental monitoring technologies
  • Public health and social care systems

These opportunities are aligned with real-world challenges and global research priorities, ensuring strong career prospects after completion.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Applicants

This opportunity is open to:

  • High-performing graduates with a bachelor’s or master’s degree
  • Early-career researchers seeking a PhD
  • Professionals transitioning into academic or research careers

Eligible Regions

  • United Kingdom applicants
  • International applicants (including Africa, Asia, Europe, and the Americas)

Most studentships are open globally, although some may have funding restrictions or preferences.

Required Qualifications

To qualify for a UWE Bristol PhD studentship, candidates typically need:

  • A first-class or upper second-class degree (or equivalent)
  • A relevant academic background aligned with the research topic
  • Strong research skills and academic writing ability
  • English language proficiency (for international applicants)
  • In some cases, a master’s degree is preferred

Applicants must demonstrate the ability to contribute meaningfully to advanced research and innovation.

Application Process Explained

Applying for a UWE Bristol studentship involves several key steps:

Step 1: Choose a Research Project

Applicants must select a specific studentship from the available list.

Step 2: Prepare Required Documents

Typical application materials include:

  • Academic transcripts
  • CV or resume
  • Personal statement or research interest statement
  • References

Step 3: Submit Application Before Deadline

Each project has a strict submission deadline.

Step 4: Shortlisting and Interview

Applicants are shortlisted and invited for an interview.

Step 5: Final Selection

Successful candidates are awarded the studentship after the interview process.

Apply here

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Studentships are highly competitive, so strong academic performance is essential
  • Some projects are tied to specific research objectives, limiting flexibility
  • Funding may have tax or benefits implications, depending on your country
  • If no suitable funded project exists, applicants can submit a self-funded proposal or apply for external funding such as doctoral loans

Why Choose UWE Bristol for a PhD?

Studying at University of the West of England offers several advantages:

  • Access to world-class research facilities
  • Collaboration with industry and global partners
  • Structured doctoral training and support
  • Strong employability outcomes after graduation

The university’s Doctoral Academy is known for fostering innovation, interdisciplinary research, and real-world impact.

Deadline

Vary depending on the specific project and faculty. Because each studentship has its own closing date, applicants must carefully track deadlines for individual projects.

The UWE Bristol PhD studentship programme is a top-tier fully funded scholarship opportunity for ambitious students worldwide. With generous funding, diverse research areas, and global eligibility, it provides a powerful pathway into academic, professional, and research careers. If you are serious about pursuing a fully funded PhD in the UK, this opportunity should be at the top of your list. Carefully review the available projects, meet the eligibility criteria, and submit your application before the relevant deadline to maximize your chances of success.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded PhD Studentships at UWE Bristol 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The University of Hong Kong (HKU) Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026: Fully Funded Visiting Fellowship for Scholars from the Global South (Hong Kong)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026 is a prestigious academic opportunity designed to foster international collaboration, research excellence, and knowledge exchange among scholars from the Global South. Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences at the University of Hong Kong (HKU), this fellowship enables researchers to engage in meaningful academic partnerships, contribute […]

The post The University of Hong Kong (HKU) Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026: Fully Funded Visiting Fellowship for Scholars from the Global South (Hong Kong) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026 is a prestigious academic opportunity designed to foster international collaboration, research excellence, and knowledge exchange among scholars from the Global South. Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences at the University of Hong Kong (HKU), this fellowship enables researchers to engage in meaningful academic partnerships, contribute to global scholarship, and strengthen cross-cultural academic networks.

This initiative reflects HKU’s commitment to internationalization, interdisciplinarity, and global impact, creating a dynamic environment where scholars can collaborate on innovative research and share insights that address pressing global challenges.

About the HKU Global South Fellowship Scheme

The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme is designed to:

  • Promote academic exchange between HKU and scholars from the Global South
  • Foster collaborative research projects with long-term impact
  • Strengthen global academic networks and partnerships
  • Encourage interdisciplinary knowledge sharing

Fellows will be hosted at HKU’s Faculty of Social Sciences and will actively participate in research, teaching, and academic engagement activities.

Fellowship Duration and Timeline

The fellowship offers flexible visiting periods aligned with HKU’s academic calendar:

  • Duration: 1 to 3 months
  • Available Periods:
    • Fall Semester: September to January
    • Spring Semester: February to June

Application Timeline

Applications are accepted twice per year, based on the intended visiting period:

Fall Semester:

  • Call for Applications: April
  • Deadline: May
  • Announcement: By end of June

Spring Semester:

  • Call for Applications: September
  • Deadline: October
  • Announcement: By end of November

Current Application Deadline:

  • May 31, 2026 (Hong Kong Time)

Fellowship Benefits and Financial Support

Selected fellows receive a comprehensive support package, including:

  1. Travel Support
    • Economy class return airfare up to HK$10,000 (reimbursable)
  2. Daily Allowance
    • HK$1,500 per day (covers accommodation and living expenses)
  3. Academic and Institutional Support
    • Access to HKU facilities
    • Opportunities to collaborate with leading academics
    • Participation in seminars, lectures, and research activities

Expected Activities and Responsibilities

Fellows are expected to actively contribute to HKU’s academic environment through:

  1. Collaborative Research
    • Work with a Faculty Associate or other HKU faculty members
    • Produce at least one co-authored publication within one year
  2. Academic Engagement
    • Deliver lectures or seminars
    • Participate in networking events and academic discussions
    • Provide training or mentorship to students
  3. Knowledge Sharing
    • Contribute to intra-fellow exchange sessions
    • Engage in interdisciplinary collaboration
  4. Reporting Requirements
    • Submit a completion report within 3 months after the fellowship
    • Share publication outcomes with the Faculty

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be professoriate staff (e.g., lecturers, assistant professors, or higher)
  • Be affiliated with a university or comparable institution
  • Be based in a Global South country
  • Demonstrate strong academic and research achievements

Preference is given to early-career or junior scholars with potential for long-term collaboration.

Why You Should Apply

The HKU Global South Fellowship offers:

  • A prestigious international academic experience
  • Opportunities for high-impact research collaboration
  • Access to a global network of scholars
  • Financial support for travel and living expenses
  • Enhanced academic visibility and career advancement

Application Process

To apply for the fellowship, applicants must follow these steps:

  1. Identify a Faculty Associate
    • Contact an HKU Faculty member with similar research interests
    • The Faculty Associate must be an Assistant Professor or above
  2. Initiate Contact
    • Send an email expressing interest in collaboration
    • Suggested subject line: “HKU FoSS Global South Fellowship Scheme”
  3. Prepare Application
    • Develop a research proposal and supporting documents
  4. Submit Application
    • Complete the official Online Application Form before the deadline

Selection Process

  • Up to 10 fellows are selected per academic year
  • Selection is based on:
    • Academic track record
    • Research achievements
    • Potential for collaboration and impact

Contact Information

For inquiries and additional support:

  • Email: fossres@hku.hk

Click HERE for more information about the HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026.

Conclusion

The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for scholars from the Global South to engage in meaningful academic exchange, collaborative research, and global knowledge production.

By participating in this fellowship, researchers can expand their academic horizons, contribute to impactful scholarship, and build long-lasting international partnerships that shape the future of social sciences.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post The University of Hong Kong (HKU) Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026: Fully Funded Visiting Fellowship for Scholars from the Global South (Hong Kong) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded AmplifyChange Grants 2026–2027: Up to £200,000 for Civil Society Organizations in Africa, Asia and the Middle East
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are part of a civil society organization working to transform communities, influence policy, and advocate for human rights, the AmplifyChange Grant Program represents one of the most impactful global funding opportunities available today. Designed to empower grassroots movements and strengthen advocacy in sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR), this funding initiative supports […]

The post Fully Funded AmplifyChange Grants 2026–2027: Up to £200,000 for Civil Society Organizations in Africa, Asia and the Middle East appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are part of a civil society organization working to transform communities, influence policy, and advocate for human rights, the AmplifyChange Grant Program represents one of the most impactful global funding opportunities available today. Designed to empower grassroots movements and strengthen advocacy in sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR), this funding initiative supports organizations that are often overlooked by traditional donors.

What Is AmplifyChange?

AmplifyChange is a global funding mechanism dedicated exclusively to supporting civil society advocacy on sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR). Its mission is to ensure that individuals everywhere can exercise their rights to health, dignity, and bodily autonomy.

Unlike traditional funding bodies, AmplifyChange prioritizes grassroots organizations, especially those operating in challenging environments where access to funding is limited. It focuses on building long-term movements, not just short-term projects.

Types of AmplifyChange Grants

AmplifyChange offers several grant categories tailored to different organizational capacities and goals:

1. Opportunity Grants

  • Funding: Up to £75,000
  • Duration: 1.5 to 3 years
  • Designed for small grassroots organizations
  • Supports local advocacy initiatives

2. Strengthening Grants

  • Funding: Up to £200,000
  • Duration: 2 to 3 years
  • Focus: Organizational growth and scaling impact
  • Allows up to 40% for capacity building

3. Network Grants

  • Supports coalitions and partnerships
  • Focuses on strengthening regional or thematic networks

4. Partnership Grants

  • Supports intermediary organizations that redistribute funding
  • Focus on long-term systems change

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants)

AmplifyChange is specifically designed for civil society organizations (CSOs). Eligible applicants include:

  • Community-based organizations
  • Youth-led groups
  • Women’s rights organizations
  • NGOs and non-profits
  • Indigenous groups
  • Faith-based organizations
  • Research institutes and social enterprises

Who Cannot Apply

  • Individuals or independent researchers
  • UN agencies
  • International NGOs based in high-income countries
  • Organizations focused solely on service delivery without advocacy

Eligible Countries and Regions

Applicants must be based in and implementing projects in eligible countries, primarily across:

  • Africa: Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Uganda, South Africa, etc.
  • South Asia: India, Nepal, Bangladesh, Pakistan
  • Middle East: Jordan, Lebanon, Palestine, Yemen

A wide list of eligible countries is provided, including Kenya, Tanzania, Ethiopia, Rwanda, and many more.

Key Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, organizations must:

  • Be registered or unregistered civil society organizations
  • Operate in an eligible country
  • Focus on advocacy (not just service delivery)
  • Align with AmplifyChange priority themes
  • Meet financial thresholds depending on grant type

For example, Opportunity Grants require:

  • Average annual expenditure below £200,000

Priority Focus Areas

Projects must align with at least one of AmplifyChange’s core SRHR themes:

  • Ending gender-based violence
  • Access to safe abortion
  • Youth sexual health and education
  • Challenging stigma and discrimination
  • Expanding access to SRHR services

These themes are central to achieving long-term policy and societal change.

Funding Benefits

Successful applicants receive:

  • Substantial financial support (up to £200,000)
  • Capacity-building resources
  • Access to global advocacy networks
  • Long-term partnership opportunities

AmplifyChange has already funded over 1,500 projects globally, disbursing more than £142 million to support SRHR initiatives.

Application Process (Step-by-Step)

Applying for AmplifyChange funding involves a structured online process:

  1. Register on the Grants Portal
  2. Complete eligibility screening questions
  3. Create organizational profile
  4. Fill out the application form
  5. Upload required documents
  6. Submit before the deadline

Important: Applications must be submitted online—late submissions are not accepted.

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

AmplifyChange stands out because it:

  • Prioritizes local voices and grassroots leadership
  • Funds advocacy and systemic change, not just services
  • Supports organizations in high-need, underfunded regions
  • Encourages innovation and intersectional approaches

It is particularly valuable for organizations seeking to influence policy, laws, and social norms around health and human rights.

Deadline

16th June 2026

The AmplifyChange Grant Program is more than just funding—it is a strategic opportunity to join a global movement advocating for justice, equality, and health rights. Whether you are a small grassroots organization or a growing NGO, this initiative provides the financial support and institutional backing needed to scale your impact. If your organization is committed to driving meaningful change in sexual and reproductive health and rights, this is an opportunity worth pursuing.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded AmplifyChange Grants 2026–2027: Up to £200,000 for Civil Society Organizations in Africa, Asia and the Middle East appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MEXT Scholarship 2027 (Embassy Recommendation): Complete Guide to All Four Application Categories for Studying in Japan
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The MEXT Scholarship 2027 (Embassy Recommendation), offered by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT), is one of the most comprehensive and prestigious fully funded scholarship programs in the world. It provides international students with the opportunity to study in Japan across multiple academic pathways—from vocational training to advanced research degrees. Administered […]

The post MEXT Scholarship 2027 (Embassy Recommendation): Complete Guide to All Four Application Categories for Studying in Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The MEXT Scholarship 2027 (Embassy Recommendation), offered by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT), is one of the most comprehensive and prestigious fully funded scholarship programs in the world. It provides international students with the opportunity to study in Japan across multiple academic pathways—from vocational training to advanced research degrees.

Administered globally through Japanese embassies and consulates with support from the Japan Student Services Organization (JASSO), this scholarship is designed to cultivate global leaders, strengthen international collaboration, and provide access to Japan’s high-quality education system.

This detailed guide focuses extensively on the four major application categories under the Embassy Recommendation route, helping applicants understand their options and choose the most suitable pathway.

Understanding the Embassy Recommendation Route

The Embassy Recommendation pathway is the most widely used application route for the MEXT Scholarship. Applicants apply through the Japanese embassy or consulate in their home country.

Key Features:

  • Applications open between April and May 2026
  • First screening takes place between May and August 2026
  • Final selection is conducted by MEXT in Japan
  • Successful candidates begin studies in April 2027 (or September/October 2027 for research students)

Important rule:
Applicants must submit documents to their local Japanese embassy or consulate, not directly to MEXT.

1. Application for Specialized Training College Students – Embassy Recommendation

This category is designed for students who wish to pursue practical, career-oriented education in Japan through specialized training colleges (Senmon Gakko).

Overview

Specialized training colleges focus on equipping students with hands-on skills and industry-relevant knowledge. These institutions are ideal for students seeking direct entry into professional careers.

Key Areas of Study

Students can choose from a wide range of vocational and technical programs, including:

  • Business and Commerce
  • Information Technology and Digital Skills
  • Fashion and Design
  • Hospitality and Tourism
  • Healthcare and Welfare
  • Engineering-related technical training

Program Structure

  • Duration typically ranges from 2 to 3 years
  • Includes practical training and internships
  • Strong emphasis on job readiness and employability

Application Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Carefully follow official application guidelines
  • Submit:
    • Application Form (FY2027)
    • Certificate of Health (completed by a licensed doctor)
    • Recommendation Letter
  • Ensure all documents are submitted to the Japanese embassy

Who This Category Is Best For

  • Students seeking practical, skill-based education
  • Individuals aiming for immediate employment after graduation
  • Applicants interested in technical or vocational careers

Learn more HERE

2. Application for College of Technology (KOSEN) Students – Embassy Recommendation

The KOSEN (Colleges of Technology) track is designed for students who want to pursue technical and engineering-focused education.

Overview

KOSEN institutions provide a unique blend of theoretical and practical engineering education, preparing students for careers in advanced industries.

Key Fields of Study

  • Mechanical Engineering
  • Electrical and Electronic Engineering
  • Civil Engineering
  • Information Technology
  • Industrial Systems Engineering

Program Structure

  • Typically a 5-year integrated program
  • Combines:
    • Classroom learning
    • Laboratory work
    • Industrial training

Learning Approach

  • Strong focus on hands-on technical skills
  • Early exposure to real-world engineering problems
  • Collaboration with industries and research institutions

Application Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Follow embassy-specific instructions
  • Submit:
    • Application Form (FY2027)
    • Certificate of Health
    • Recommendation Letter

Who This Category Is Best For

  • Students passionate about engineering and technology
  • Individuals interested in applied sciences and innovation
  • Those seeking early specialization in technical fields

Learn more HERE

3. Application for Undergraduate Students – Embassy Recommendation

This category is for students who wish to pursue a full bachelor’s degree at a Japanese university.

Overview

The undergraduate track is one of the most popular MEXT Scholarship categories, offering access to top-ranked universities in Japan.

Fields of Study

Applicants can choose from diverse disciplines:

  • Natural Sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Biology)
  • Engineering and Technology
  • Social Sciences (Economics, Political Science)
  • Humanities (History, Literature, Languages)
  • Business and Management

Program Structure

  • Duration: 4–5 years (including preparatory year if needed)
  • Preparatory education may include:
    • Japanese language training
    • Foundational academic courses

Additional Application Components

Applicants may be required to submit:

  • Direct Placement Preference Form
  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • Entrance examination results (if applicable)

Application Requirements

  • Strong academic performance
  • Completion of secondary education
  • Submission of:
    • Application Form
    • Certificate of Health
    • Recommendation Letter

Who This Category Is Best For

  • High school graduates seeking full university education abroad
  • Students aiming for academic and professional careers
  • Individuals interested in broad academic disciplines

Learn more HERE

4. Application for Research Students – Embassy Recommendation

The Research Student category is designed for postgraduate applicants pursuing Master’s, PhD, or independent research.

Overview

This is the most advanced category, offering flexibility for students to engage in academic research under Japanese universities and supervisors.

Key Features

  • Opportunity to:
    • Conduct independent research
    • Enroll in Master’s or Doctoral programs
  • Flexible start dates:
    • April 2027
    • September/October 2027

Required Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Application Form (FY2027)
  • Placement Preference Form
  • Field of Study and Research Plan
  • Certificate of Health
  • Recommendation Letter

Research Proposal Importance

A strong research plan must include:

  • Clear research objectives
  • Defined methodology
  • Expected outcomes
  • Relevance to academic or societal challenges

Fields of Research

  • Science and Technology
  • Social Sciences and Policy
  • Environmental Studies
  • Health and Medical Research
  • Humanities and Cultural Studies

Who This Category Is Best For

  • Graduates pursuing Master’s or PhD degrees
  • Researchers seeking international collaboration
  • Academics interested in advanced research opportunities

Learn more HERE

General Application Documents (All Categories)

All applicants must prepare:

  1. Application Form (FY2027)
  2. Certificate of Health (completed by a medical doctor)
  3. Recommendation Letter (flexible format accepted)

Documents can be:

  • Handwritten
  • Typed
  • Completed digitally and printed

Deadline

Applications are typically open between April and May 2026, but exact deadlines vary by country. Applicants must confirm deadlines with their respective Japanese embassy.

Conclusion

The MEXT Scholarship 2027 (Embassy Recommendation) offers four distinct pathways tailored to different academic and career goals:

  • Specialized Training College: Career-focused vocational education
  • KOSEN: Technical and engineering training
  • Undergraduate: Full university degrees
  • Research: Advanced academic and research opportunities

Choosing the right category is critical to aligning your academic background and future ambitions with Japan’s education system.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post MEXT Scholarship 2027 (Embassy Recommendation): Complete Guide to All Four Application Categories for Studying in Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Football Innovation Analyst Job at Right to Dream: A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Football Performance
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The world of football is rapidly evolving, driven by innovation, data, and cutting-edge performance strategies. If you are passionate about transforming how football is played and analyzed, the Football Innovation Analyst role at Right to Dream (RTD) offers a unique, career-defining opportunity. This position places you at the heart of elite football development, where creativity […]

The post Football Innovation Analyst Job at Right to Dream: A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Football Performance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The world of football is rapidly evolving, driven by innovation, data, and cutting-edge performance strategies. If you are passionate about transforming how football is played and analyzed, the Football Innovation Analyst role at Right to Dream (RTD) offers a unique, career-defining opportunity.

This position places you at the heart of elite football development, where creativity meets analytics to produce real, measurable impact on the pitch. With a presence spanning multiple continents, Right to Dream continues to redefine talent development and performance excellence.

 

About Right to Dream

Right to Dream is a globally recognized football organization dedicated to developing young talent through academies and professional clubs. Operating across countries such as Denmark, Egypt, and Ghana, RTD combines education, sport, and innovation to create future football leaders.

Their approach goes beyond traditional training by integrating data-driven insights, modern coaching techniques, and a strong commitment to personal development.

 

Job Overview: Football Innovation Analyst

The Football Innovation Analyst role is designed for forward-thinking professionals who thrive at the intersection of:

  • Football performance
  • Data analytics
  • Tactical innovation
  • Research and development

This is not a traditional analyst role. Instead, it is a strategic and hands-on position focused on embedding innovation into real football environments.

 

Location and Mobility

  • Location: Flexible
  • Travel: International travel required
  • Key Countries Involved:
    • Denmark
    • Egypt
    • Ghana

The role requires spending 3–4 periods per year on-site within academies and clubs to ensure effective implementation of strategies and to build strong working relationships.

 

Key Responsibilities

As a Football Innovation Analyst, your work will directly influence team performance and player development. Your responsibilities include:

1. Leading Innovation in Set-Piece Performance

  • Drive research and innovation in set-piece strategies across RTD academies and clubs
  • Identify performance gaps and introduce improvements

2. Collaborating with Technical Teams

  • Work closely with coaches, analysts, and technical staff
  • Define key performance questions and priorities

3. Performance Analysis and Strategy Development

  • Analyze existing routines and tactical setups
  • Design and test new strategies to enhance match outcomes

4. Translating Insights into Action

  • Convert complex data and research into practical, easy-to-implement solutions
  • Ensure insights lead to tangible improvements on the pitch

5. Cross-Club Knowledge Sharing

  • Act as a connector across RTD environments
  • Share best practices and innovations between academies and clubs

6. Advanced Data and AI Utilization

  • Use performance data, video analysis, and AI-supported tools
  • Stay ahead of emerging trends in football analytics

7. Match and Opponent Analysis

  • Conduct detailed match reviews and opponent scouting when required

8. Long-Term Research and Development

  • Explore global football trends
  • Focus on marginal gains that create competitive advantages

 

Minimum Requirements

To be successful in this role, candidates must meet the following criteria:

Professional Experience

  • At least 3 years of experience in:
    • Football analysis
    • Data analytics
    • Performance research

Technical Expertise

  • Strong experience using:
    • Performance data
    • Video analysis tools
    • AI-supported technologies

Football Environment Exposure

  • Experience working in:
    • Elite academies
    • Professional football clubs
    • High-performance environments

Innovation and Strategy

  • Proven ability to:
    • Design and implement performance initiatives
    • Drive innovation in sports environments

Core Skills

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Cultural awareness and adaptability
  • Curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset

 

Reporting Structure

  • Reports to: Group Head of Football – RTD Group

This ensures alignment with the organization’s strategic vision and performance objectives.

 

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This role is more than just a job—it is a chance to:

  • Work at the forefront of football innovation and analytics
  • Collaborate with elite coaches and technical leaders
  • Influence real match outcomes and player development
  • Operate in a global, multicultural environment
  • Contribute to shaping the future of football performance

 

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 18th May 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early to maximize their chances.

 

How to Apply

Candidates who are passionate about football innovation and meet the requirements are invited to apply through the official Right to Dream careers platform.

Apply here

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Football Innovation Analyst Job at Right to Dream: A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Football Performance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
